title: salix repens subsp. brachypoda
intro: Salix repens subsp. brachypoda, also known as dwarf willow or creeping willow, is a subspecies of Salix repens, a type of willow tree native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are due to the presence of salicylic acid, a compound also found in aspirin. The health benefits of Salix repens subsp. brachypoda include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating wounds, as well as being used to treat digestive issues and as a natural diuretic. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds, or taken internally to treat a variety of health issues.
